subject: Biology
title: The Mariana Trench
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Mariana Trench usable.

hook.text: The Mariana Trench is nearly 11 km deep. If Mount Everest were placed inside, its peak would still be underwater.

buildup.text: Challenger Deep, the lowest point, has pressure over 1,000 times that at the surface.

discovery.text: In 1960, Jacques Piccard and Don Walsh descended in the Trieste. They saw a flatfish at the bottom.

twist.text: Researchers found a plastic bag at the bottom of the trench—human pollution reaches even the deepest point.

climax.text: Amphipods living at these depths have evolved unique pressure-resistant proteins to survive.

punchline.text: The deepest place on Earth. Not empty. Not untouched.

quiz.question: How deep is the Mariana Trench's Challenger Deep?
